title: monanthes minima subsp. minima
intro: Monanthes minima subsp. minima, also known as the dwarf false roselle, is a low-growing, succulent plant belonging to the Acanthaceae family. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and ease pain. The health benefits of Monanthes minima subsp. minima include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make ointments and creams for topical application.
